summary:
Ceftazidime and cefoperazone have documented activity against *Pseudomonas aeruginosa*. Among common cephalosporins, these two classes stand out due to their specific efficacy against this organism. 

Final answer: $\boxed{\text{Cefoperazone}}$  

Note: The original query did not specify which option was selected—but based on standard multiple-choice questions involving such agents, selecting an actual known active drug aligns with best practice.

(End of Round)

The correct cephalosporins active against *Pseudomonas aeruginosa* include ceftazidime and cefoperazone, among others. Of those commonly evaluated, cefoperazone specifically demonstrates proven effectiveness against this pathogen.  
$\boxed{\text{Cefoperazone}}$
